2606.03322.title: Multi-Modal Graph Neural Network with Transformer-Guided Adaptive Diffusion for Preclinical Alzheimer Classification
2606.03322.paper_summary: The graphical representation of the brain offers critical insights into diagnosing and prognosing neurodegenerative disease via relationships between regions of interest (ROIs). Despite recent emergence of various Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) to effectively capture the relational information, there remain inherent limitations in interpreting the brain networks. Specifically, convolutional approaches ineffectively aggregate information from distant neighborhoods, while attention-based methods exhibit deficiencies in capturing node-centric information, particularly in retaining critical characteristics from pivotal nodes. These shortcomings reveal challenges for identifying disease-specific variation from diverse features from different modalities. In this regard, we propose an integrated framework guiding diffusion process at each node by a downstream transformer where both short- and long-range properties of graphs are aggregated via diffusion-kernel and multi-head attention respectively. We demonstrate the superiority of our model by improving performance of pre-clinical Alzheimer's disease (AD) classification with various modalities. Also, our model adeptly identifies key ROIs that are closely associated with the preclinical stages of AD, marking a significant potential for early diagnosis and prevision of the disease.
2606.03322.paper_summary_zh: 腦部的圖形表示提供了關鍵的見解，用於通過感興趣區域 (ROIs) 之間的關係來診斷和預測神經退行性疾病。儘管最近出現了各種圖神經網絡 (GNNs) 以有效捕捉關係信息，但在解釋腦網絡方面仍然存在固有的限制。具體而言，卷積方法無法有效地聚合來自遙遠鄰域的信息，而基於注意力的方法在捕捉以節點為中心的信息方面存在不足，特別是在保留關鍵節點的關鍵特徵方面。這些缺陷揭示了從不同模態的多樣特徵中識別疾病特定變異的挑戰。在這方面，我們提出了一個綜合框架，通過下游Transformer引導每個節點的擴散過程，其中圖的短距離和長距離特性分別通過擴散核和多頭注意力進行聚合。我們通過提高各種模態的臨床前阿茲海默病 (AD) 分類性能來展示我們模型的優越性。此外，我們的模型熟練地識別與AD臨床前階段密切相關的關鍵ROIs，標誌著早期診斷和預測該疾病的重大潛力。
2606.03322.author: Jaeyoon Sim et.al.
2606.03322.authors: Jaeyoon Sim, Minjae Lee, Guorong Wu, Won Hwa Kim
